user_message: Just came out of escalation triage with Litware Inc. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-01-07
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft), David (Litware Inc)

[00:01:15] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is escalation triage.

[00:06:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:08:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] David (Litware Inc): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] David (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:26:00] David (Litware Inc): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-N trap (Follow-up + decision-log + MSX-proposal as separate artifacts creates context-sw); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-N failure mode (Follow-up + decision-log + MSX-proposal as separate artifacts creates context-sw)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-N -- Follow-up + decision-log + MSX-proposal as separate artifacts creates context-sw
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
